user.title: Set up Wholesale EV Schedule
user.description: Give this instance a unique name if you're setting up more than one (e.g. per car) — it becomes the entity_id prefix. Pick which service supplies your actual and forecast wholesale prices; "Custom" lets you point at an unsupported source directly. Scheduling tolerances (gamble tolerance, block length, max price) and the manual charge override are set afterwards as live entities, not here.

data_description.update_interval_minutes: How often prices are re-fetched and the full schedule recomputed. Charging desired also re-evaluates every minute on the clock regardless of this setting, so on/off transitions always land within about a minute of the real slot boundary.

rates_octopus_energy.title: Octopus Energy rates
rates_octopus_energy.description: Find these under Settings → Devices & services → Octopus Energy on your HA instance.

rates_custom.title: Custom actual-rates source
rates_custom.description: Point at any entity exposing an attribute holding a list of rate points, and tell us the key names within each point.

data.rate_unit_multiplier: Price multiplier (e.g. 100 to convert £/kWh to p/kWh)

forecast_agile_predict.title: AgilePredict-compatible forecast
forecast_agile_predict.description: The REST sensor entity you created pointing at an AgilePredict-compatible API for your region (e.g. AgilePredict or Agile Forecast — see agilepredict.com/v2/api_how_to for the HA sensor snippet; any source exposing the same response shape works here).

forecast_custom.title: Custom forecast source
forecast_custom.description: Point at any entity exposing an attribute holding a list of forecast points, and tell us the key names within each point.

data.forecast_unit_multiplier: Price multiplier (1 if already in the same unit as your actual rates)

abort.already_configured: An instance with this name (or its slugified entity_id prefix) is already configured. Choose a different name.

data_description.update_interval_minutes: How often prices are re-fetched and the full schedule recomputed. Charging desired also re-evaluates every minute on the clock regardless of this setting, so on/off transitions always land within about a minute of the real slot boundary.

data.rate_unit_multiplier: Price multiplier (e.g. 100 to convert £/kWh to p/kWh)

data.forecast_unit_multiplier: Price multiplier (1 if already in the same unit as your actual rates)
